## prompt-final-passage

*Generated by Process A from the submitted `prompts/prompt-enactment.md`, adapted
from the prior build's `adoption/sub-prompts/prompt-4-final-framework.md` and the
final-paper polish of `adoption/final-framework`. This build is analogous to, and
does not rewrite, the prior adoption framework.*

Write the final-framework into `law-physical-ai-trials/enactment/final-framework/`
by carrying the verify-framework to publication quality. The table-and-figure audit
has already been certified in `enactment/verify-framework/`; this stage applies the
remaining senior-author finishing and produces the submission-ready manuscript,
including the `final-framework/publication/` subdirectory with the deposit-ready
source.

**Corrections to identify and apply (senior-author finishing).**

- White space: remove large empty bands; where interword spacing looks uneven, tune
  `\RaggedRight`, `\emergencystretch`, and `\tolerance` so words are evenly spaced
  with no right-margin overflow; use `\raggedbottom` so page bottoms are natural.
- Pagination: no orphan or widow line, no one or two word last line, no single line
  stranded onto the next page; balance each page with `\clearpage`, `\vspace`, and
  `\hspace`.
- Tables: carry forward the verify stage's optimized column widths; every table
  matches the body measure exactly at `\textwidth`, every column is
  `>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{<width>}`, referenced with `\autoref`; add a
  ninth synthesis table mapping each of the eight questions to the bill provision
  or evidence that answers it.
- Figures: carry forward the verify stage's certified figures unchanged in geometry
  (the twice-verified routing, looseness, and spacing); keep the uniform
  `\vspace{-0.75cm}` between every figure and its caption; keep strictly to black,
  grayscales, `#4B0082`, `#000080`, `#C0C0C0`; refine only captions so they explain
  rather than narrate.
- Symbols: every section reference uses "§", never "SS"; single hyphens only (no en,
  em, double, or triple dashes); straight quotes consistent with the template.
- Prose: definitions precede first use; ideas connect across sections; each passage
  question closes by pointing the legislator at the specific provision, score line,
  or evidence that answers it; the recurring questions resolve into a clear vote.

**Sources.** Same evidence base as the full stage
(`enactment/references/author_works.bib`, `enactment/references/passage_refs.bib`,
the bill text and platform scope, the bill DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20619762), now cited
cleanly with the `ieeetr` style and `\nocite{*}` so every entry renders and each DOI
or URL prints its clickable resolver. The author reference set is trimmed to the
works that bear on legislation, regulation, and the platform; topic references carry
working URLs to professional sources and journals.

One commit each for `main.tex`, the `.sty`, `references.bib`, and `README.md`; one
commit per `sections/*.tex` file; one commit each for the `publication/` mirror;
plus `prompt-final-passage.md` and `output-final-passage.md`. For the
second-to-last commit fix all errors across all files; for the last commit write the
directory README and the `final-framework-LaTeX.zip` Overleaf bundle and refresh the
pull request. The manuscript must compile in Overleaf with pdfLaTeX and preserve the
`#4B0082` template visual theme. After this stage, the single v0.3.0 release update
follows (root `CHANGELOG.md`, `releases.md`, and the main `README.md` with its added
version section, badges, ASCII and colored Mermaid diagrams, and repository
structure), without rewriting the prior v0.1.0 and v0.2.0 content. Commit and push
in real time; do not hold commits. Do not stall, ask questions, or go into plan
mode.
